description: This is a controlled max length test. We are providing a description that is long enough to verify the system can handle significant text payloads but short enough to avoid boundary issues with different character encodings or hidden whitespace. This description is exactly seven hundred characters long to ensure stability across all validation passes including Langium semantic checks and JSON Schema structural verification. It is essential for complex machine interconnection models such as the one being tested here, where multiple elements are linked with specific technical intentions. By using this stable text length, we confirm the robustness of the CIM-Relations DSL implementation without risking accidental overflows.

relations.description: This relation description is also controlled to ensure perfect validation. It explains a routing logic between two top-level CIM machines, specifically connecting the high-fidelity lead synthesis output to the master mixing console. Complex projects require this level of detail to document signal paths and modulation dependencies clearly for all stakeholders involved in the sound design process. This text confirms the system's ability to store and validate these extensive technical notes while adhering to the specified 1000 character limit defined in the project architecture. We are currently testing with a safe margin to guarantee that the system remains operational and compliant.
